commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of guarded-transport compatibility risk, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No supply-chain, secret-handling, workflow, lockfile, or new third-party execution concern is visible in the diff. (sha=61df22356358ddb7c8c006ec6f20a19d09892791)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wires `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` into the OpenAI Responses SDK client and adds constructor plus guard-specific SSRF-block tests for that path.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +135. Total +137 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main lacks the OpenAI Responses SDK `fetch` hook, and the pinned OpenAI SDK uses the configured client fetch for Responses requests. I did not establish a live oversized-stream reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of guarded-transport compatibility risk, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
"## What Problem This Solves

The OpenAI Responses provider (`openai-responses.ts`) creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option. All streaming Responses API calls from this provider bypass the guarded provider transport — SSRF protection, request timeout, retry-hint injection, and response lifecycle management that every other OpenAI SDK path already has.

This injects `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` into the SDK constructor, bringing the Responses provider to guarded-fetch parity with:
- `openai-transport-stream.ts` (3 branches)
- `openai-completions.ts` (#97228)
- `azure-openai-responses.ts`

## Why This Change Was Made

`buildGuardedModelFetch` is the canonical fetch wrapper applied to every OpenAI SDK constructor in the codebase. The OpenAI SDK accepts `fetch?: Fetch | undefined` per its constructor options. The Responses provider was the remaining OpenAI SDK path without this wrapper.

The change is 2 lines (+1 import, +1 `fetch:` option) — the same scope as #97228 for completions.

## Evidence

### Layer 1: Mock-based constructor wiring

`openai-responses.test.ts` verifies the SDK constructor receives `buildGuardedModelFetch`:

```
 ✓ wires buildGuardedModelFetch into the OpenAI SDK fetch option
```

**Negative control**: removing `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` makes `typeof captured.fetch === "function"` fail.

### Layer 2: Real HTTP pipeline interception

`openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts` stubs `globalThis.fetch` without mocking the `openai` SDK and verifies the SDK's POST to `/v1/responses` routes through `buildGuardedModelFetch`:

| Field | Captured value |
|-------|---------------|
| URL | `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses` |
| Method | POST |
| Model | `gpt-5.5` |
| Stream | `true` |
| Input | Messages API format (`{type:"message", role:"user", content:[{type:"input_text", text:"hi"}]}`) |

The SDK retries 2-3x on 500; every call targets the same endpoint.

### Test results

```
✓ 2 tests passed (2 files)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts             — 1 test (new)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts — 1 test (new)
```

### Diff scope

```
3 files changed, 165 insertions(+)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts                     |  2 ++
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts                | 75 ++++++
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts    | 88 ++++++
```

### Verification commands

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts \
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts
# 2 passed ✓

npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json \
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts \
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts
# No errors ✓
```

## Security & Privacy

- Hardening change: previously-unwrapped OpenAI Responses SDK fetch calls now inherit OpenClaw's SSRF guard, request timeout, retry hints, and response lifecycle management.
- No new network calls, no new persisted data, no new logging of secrets.
- The `fetch` option is standard OpenAI SDK API surface; no monkey-patching.

## Compatibility

- Existing OpenAI Responses API requests now use guarded fetch. For standard `api.openai.com` endpoints this is transparent.
- Custom `baseUrl` (enterprise proxy, self-hosted gateway) now gets the same SSRF policy as other OpenAI SDK paths.
- No config/env changes, no migration needed.
- Blast radius: one runtime file + its tests. No shared mocks, no public API.

## What was not tested

- The 16 MiB JSON-synthesis cap and 64 KiB non-OK cap are exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`.
- No live OpenAI endpoint was hit (the fetch-intercept test proves the SDK uses our fetch).
"
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
